title: USD Reference Namespace Source Contract

included: wrapping a base NamespaceSource with bounded reference composition
reading source-visible references fields from base and referenced prim inputs
path-only internal references within the base layer-stack namespace
external reference arcs using already-opened supplied reference layers
asset-only reference target selection through referenced layer defaultPrim
mapping reference target prims and descendants under the referring prim path
bounded recursive or chained reference expansion across supplied referenced layers
composed namespace mapping through chained reference targets
remapping path-valued field contents authored under referenced specs into the referring namespace
local opinions stronger than referenced opinions
same-reference-family strength ordering by namespace depth and source-visible authored list order
reference-family-only prim/property opinion views or equivalent separable provenance for downstream composition consumers
arc-local layerRelocates authored in referenced layer stacks and applied to references introduced from those layer stacks
recoverable ancestral-relocate diagnostics for invalid nested relocate source paths in referenced layer stacks
effective population specifier selection across local and referenced specs
ordered child prim name composition across local and referenced specs
ordered property name composition across local and referenced specs
diagnostics for missing referenced layers, missing target prims, and recursive reference cycles
deferred: reference asset resolution and resource opening
resolver search paths, callbacks, contexts, and repository policy
payload loading and payload load policy
inherits, specializes, variants, root-layer relocates, and value clips
attribute value resolution, time sample interpolation, and layer offsets

model.ReferenceNamespaceSource: A namespace-source provider backed by a base NamespaceSource plus already-opened referenced Layers or single-layer roots of referenced layer stacks.
model.BaseNamespaceSource: The stronger local namespace input, usually produced by usd-layer-stack-namespace-source.
model.ReferenceArc: A source-visible Reference item from a prim's references field, with optional asset, optional target path, offset, and scale.
model.ReferenceLayerSet: Already-opened document-model Layers supplied by authored reference asset identifier. A richer implementation may supply layer stacks, but this bounded contract only requires a layer root per asset identifier.
model.ReferenceMapping: The target prim path in a referenced layer mapped to the referring prim path in the base namespace.
model.RecursiveReferenceClosure: The finite set of direct and nested reference arcs reachable from source-visible referenced target subtrees using supplied already-opened layers.
model.ContributingSources: Adapter rendering of strong-to-weak ContributingOpinion records. Domain records store source identity separately from semantic spec paths.

input.referenceLayers: already-opened document-model Layers or bounded layer-stack roots keyed by authored reference asset identifier

output.references: reference arcs in the bounded closure with primPath, asset, targetPath, offset, and scale

output.exists: whether base or referenced specs contribute a prim-like input at the path
output.childNames: composed child prim names before stage mask or active filtering
output.propertyNames: composed property names before schema fallback injection

output.exists: whether base or referenced specs contribute an attribute or relationship at the path
output.fields: strongest authored field per field key

input.path: absolute prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.opinions: strong-to-weak base and referenced scene-object opinions before variant evaluation

laws: the reference source consumes a base NamespaceSource and must not require stage population to know whether opinions came from local layers or references
the reference source consumes supplied document-model Layers for referenced assets and must not open files or resolve assets
reference source records must retain referring prim paths, referenced target paths, and mapped contributor paths as semantic path handles/references rather than raw strings
reference contributingSources domain data must be typed ContributingOpinion records, not composite source:path strings
local base-source opinions are stronger than referenced opinions
when multiple reference arcs contribute to the same exposed scene path, reference arcs authored deeper in namespace are stronger than reference arcs authored higher in namespace
when multiple sibling reference arcs authored at the same prim contribute to the same exposed scene path, source-visible authored list order is preserved as strong-to-weak order
a referenced target prim maps to the referring prim path, and descendants are mapped by replacing the target prefix with the referring prim path

nested references authored inside supplied referenced target subtrees must be expanded transitively while referenced layers are available; each nested reference maps into the exposed namespace by composing the parent reference mapping with the nested reference target mapping

when a nested reference is authored inside a referenced layer stack that has layerRelocates, the nested reference record must carry that layer stack's relocate namespace mapping in addition to the normal reference target mapping

arc-local relocate mappings on reference records must hide source paths, map relocated target paths back to the remote source specs, suppress stale target collisions from the referenced target layer, and preserve target-local opinions from the relocating layer as stronger than relocated source opinions

childNames and propertyNames emitted by a reference source must be derived from mapped target paths, not copied directly from referenced layer tokens, so relocate targets, deletes, and nested relocate descendants are reflected in the exposed namespace
recursive reference expansion must retain each contributing opinion as a reference-family opinion with typed provenance until the composition arbitrator applies LIVERPS arbitration

the reference source must preserve enough separable reference-family opinion data for downstream providers, such as relocates, to consume referenced opinions at a path without also consuming local base-source opinions at that same path

recursive reference expansion must detect cycles by authored asset identifier, referenced target path, referring prim path, and accumulated namespace mapping context; cycles must be diagnosed and must not recurse unboundedly or crash generated implementations

path-valued field contents authored on referenced prims or properties, including listOp<ObjectPath> targetPaths and connectionPaths, must be remapped by replacing the referenced target prefix with the referring prim path before the fields are emitted

referenced targetPaths and connectionPaths elements outside the referenced target subtree are pruned in this bounded external-reference provider instead of being emitted in referenced-layer coordinates

for path-only internal references within the same layer-stack namespace, targetPaths and connectionPaths elements outside the referenced target subtree remain in the shared composed namespace rather than being pruned

for recursive external references, path-valued field remapping must use the accumulated namespace mapping; out-of-subtree elements are pruned unless the arc is same-namespace and already in composed coordinates

for recursive external references with arc-local relocates, path-valued field remapping must use the accumulated reference mapping plus the arc-local relocate mapping; paths inside deleted relocate sources are pruned
path-valued field remapping is namespace composition and does not imply full listOp composition, dictionary merging, value resolution, or layer-offset application
asset-only references use the referenced layer defaultPrim; a missing defaultPrim must be diagnosed
a missing supplied reference layer must be diagnosed with the authored asset identifier
a missing referenced target prim must be diagnosed with the authored asset and target path
reference layer offsets are reported but are not applied to time samples, splines, clips, timecode-valued fields, or attribute values
for non-special field keys in this first contract, field maps expose the strongest authored field value and do not combine dictionaries, listOps, time samples, or values
childNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring child_ordering=composed
propertyNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring property_ordering=composed
adapter JSON may seed base and referenced layers for conformance tests, but domain APIs exchange NamespaceSource, Layer, path, token, and field value objects

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with rootLayer, optional sublayers map, optional referenceLayers map, and an ops array. The adapter constructs a base layer-stack namespace source before constructing the reference namespace source.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
